title: Getting started with rcol
text: Almost every function takes dataset = argument identifying checklist work . can pass: numeric dataset key, e.g. 3 (COL project), dataset alias "COL25" (2025 annual release), one magic COL release aliases always resolve recent release. COL published two cadences (monthly annual) two flavours: base release extended release (XR) merges additional data. clb_col_release() returns right identifier: package default "3LXR", latest monthly extended release.
code: clb_col_release("monthly")                  # "3LR"   latest monthly base release clb_col_release("monthly", extended = TRUE) # "3LXR"  latest monthly extended release clb_col_release("annual")                   # e.g. "COL25"  # Every release, e.g. to find historical annual editions clb_col_releases()

title: Getting started with rcol
text: clb_match() returns single best match — COL analogue GBIF’s name backbone matching: Use clb_match_verbose() see candidate usages, clb_match_checklist() match whole vector data frame parallel:
code: clb_match("Panthera leo") clb_match("Abies alba", rank = "species", code = "botanical") clb_match_verbose("Oenanthe")  clb_match_checklist(c("Panthera leo", "Bufo bufo", "Abies alba"))

title: Getting started with rcol
text: ChecklistBank parsers exposed — scientific name parser controlled-value parsers:
code: clb_parse_name("Abies alba Mill.")  clb_parsers() clb_parse("rank", c("spec", "fam.", "ssp"))

title: Getting started with rcol
Getting started with rcol
code: # Look up a usage and its context clb_usage("4CGXP", dataset = "3LR") clb_classification("4CGXP", dataset = "3LR") clb_synonyms("6DBT", dataset = "3LR") clb_vernacular(id = "4CGXP", dataset = "3LR")  # Navigate the tree roots <- clb_tree(dataset = "3LR") clb_children(roots$id[1], dataset = "3LR")  # Full-text search clb_usage_search("Felidae", dataset = "3LR") clb_suggest("Panth", dataset = "3LR")

title: Getting started with rcol
text: Set CLB_BASE_URL target development API, pass server = clb_match() match locally running dockerized matching container:
code: Sys.setenv(CLB_BASE_URL = "https://api.dev.checklistbank.org")  # docker run -p 8080:8080 docker.gbif.org/matching-ws:xcol-amd64-latest clb_match("Panthera leo", server = "http://localhost:8080")

title: Authors and Citation
text: Markus Döring. Author, maintainer. Catalogue Life. Copyright holder, funder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Döring M (2026). rcol: R Client Catalogue Life / ChecklistBank API. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/CatalogueOfLife/rcol.
code: @Manual{,   title = {rcol: R Client for the Catalogue of Life / ChecklistBank API},   author = {Markus Döring},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/CatalogueOfLife/rcol}, }

what: R Client for the Catalogue of Life / ChecklistBank API
title: R Client for the Catalogue of Life / ChecklistBank API
text: rcol R client Catalogue Life via ChecklistBank API (https://api.checklistbank.org). lets match scientific names, look datasets, taxa name usages, navigate taxonomic tree, run ChecklistBank parsers — public dataset ChecklistBank latest Catalogue Life release. modelled conventions rgbif taxadb.

title: R Client for the Catalogue of Life / ChecklistBank API
text: Install development version GitHub: Documentation published https://catalogueoflife.github.io/rcol/.

title: R Client for the Catalogue of Life / ChecklistBank API
code: library(rcol)  # Match a name against the latest extended COL release (the default dataset) clb_match("Panthera leo")  # Match many names at once clb_match_checklist(c("Panthera leo", "Bufo bufo", "Abies alba"))  # Parse a scientific name into its components clb_parse_name("Abies alba Mill.")

title: R Client for the Catalogue of Life / ChecklistBank API
text: Catalogue Life published two cadences (monthly annual) two flavours (base release extended release XR). Every function takes dataset = argument; helpful aliases resolve latest :
code: clb_col_release("monthly")                  # "3LR"   latest monthly base clb_col_release("monthly", extended = TRUE) # "3LXR"  latest monthly extended (default) clb_col_release("annual")                   # e.g. "COL25"  clb_usage_search("Felidae", dataset = "COL25")

title: R Client for the Catalogue of Life / ChecklistBank API
code: # Search datasets clb_dataset_search("reptile")  # Walk the tree roots <- clb_tree(dataset = "3LR") clb_children(roots$id[1], dataset = "3LR")  # Classification and synonyms of a taxon clb_classification("4CGXP", dataset = "3LR") clb_synonyms("6DBT", dataset = "3LR")  # Metrics clb_dataset_metrics() clb_usage_metrics("4CGXP", dataset = "3LR")

title: R Client for the Catalogue of Life / ChecklistBank API
text: base URL defaults production API can redirected CLB_BASE_URL environment variable, e.g. development server locally running dockerized matching container:

title: R Client for the Catalogue of Life / ChecklistBank API
text: high-volume offline matching, Catalogue Life publishes dockerized matching containers bundle release expose matching service locally:

title: R Client for the Catalogue of Life / ChecklistBank API
text: MIT © Catalogue Life

what: The ChecklistBank API base URL — clb_base_url
title: The ChecklistBank API base URL — clb_base_url
text: Returns base URL requests sent . Defaults public production API can overridden CLB_BASE_URL environment variable, e.g. target development server (https://api.dev.checklistbank.org) locally running dockerized matching container (http://localhost:8080).

title: The ChecklistBank API base URL — clb_base_url
The ChecklistBank API base URL — clb_base_url
text: length-one character vector base URL.

title: The ChecklistBank API base URL — clb_base_url

what: Direct children of a taxon — clb_children
title: Direct children of a taxon — clb_children
text: Lists immediate child taxa node taxonomic tree.

title: Direct children of a taxon — clb_children
code: clb_children(   id,   dataset = "3LXR",   extinct = TRUE,   ...,   limit = 100L,   max = limit,   .raw = FALSE )

title: Direct children of a taxon — clb_children
text: id Taxon id within dataset. dataset Dataset key alias. Defaults "3LXR". extinct Logical; include extinct taxa. Defaults TRUE. ... query parameters (e.g. insertPlaceholder). limit Page size per request. max Maximum number children return. Use Inf fetch . .raw Return raw parsed JSON instead tibble?

title: Direct children of a taxon — clb_children
text: tibble child tree nodes.

title: Direct children of a taxon — clb_children

what: Taxonomic classification (lineage) of a taxon — clb_classification
title: Taxonomic classification (lineage) of a taxon — clb_classification
text: Returns ordered parent chain root taxon.

title: Taxonomic classification (lineage) of a taxon — clb_classification
Taxonomic classification (lineage) of a taxon — clb_classification
text: id Taxon id within dataset. dataset Dataset key alias. Defaults "3LXR". .raw Return raw parsed JSON instead tibble?

title: Taxonomic classification (lineage) of a taxon — clb_classification
text: tibble ancestors, one row per rank, columns id, name, authorship, rank labelHtml.

title: Taxonomic classification (lineage) of a taxon — clb_classification

what: Resolve a Catalogue of Life release — clb_col_release
title: Resolve a Catalogue of Life release — clb_col_release
text: Catalogue Life published managed project (dataset key 3) immutable releases derived. two cadences (monthly annual) two flavours (base release extended release XR, merges additional external data). helper returns dataset identifier (alias "3LXR" "COL25") can pass dataset = argument package.

title: Resolve a Catalogue of Life release — clb_col_release
Resolve a Catalogue of Life release — clb_col_release
text: cadence Either "monthly" (default) "annual". extended Logical. Return extended release (XR) instead base release? Defaults FALSE.

title: Resolve a Catalogue of Life release — clb_col_release
text: length-one character vector release alias, NA (warning) requested release published yet – currently case annual extended release.

title: Resolve a Catalogue of Life release — clb_col_release
text: monthly releases stable magic aliases always point recent one: "3LR" – latest monthly base release "3LXR" – latest monthly extended release Annual releases use COL<YY> alias (e.g. "COL25", "COL25 XR" extended variant). latest one discovered list releases project 3.

title: Resolve a Catalogue of Life release — clb_col_release
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ clb_col_release("monthly")                  # "3LR" clb_col_release("monthly", extended = TRUE) # "3LXR" clb_col_release("annual")                   # e.g. "COL25" } # }

what: List all Catalogue of Life releases — clb_col_releases
title: List all Catalogue of Life releases — clb_col_releases
text: Returns every release (base extended) derived Catalogue Life project (dataset key 3), useful discovering historical annual releases COL23, COL24, COL25.

title: List all Catalogue of Life releases — clb_col_releases
List all Catalogue of Life releases — clb_col_releases
text: project Integer project key. Defaults 3 (Catalogue Life). ... Additional query parameters passed /dataset endpoint.

title: List all Catalogue of Life releases — clb_col_releases
text: tibble releases columns key, alias, title, origin, issued version.

title: List all Catalogue of Life releases — clb_col_releases
Get dataset metadata — clb_dataset
text: Retrieves full metadata record single dataset.

title: Get dataset metadata — clb_dataset
Get dataset metadata — clb_dataset
text: key Dataset key alias (e.g. 3, "3LXR", "COL25"). .raw Return raw parsed JSON instead tibble?

title: Get dataset metadata — clb_dataset
text: one-row tibble dataset metadata (nested fields contact creator list-columns), raw list .raw = TRUE.

title: Get dataset metadata — clb_dataset
Dataset metrics — clb_dataset_metrics
text: Returns record counts dataset's recent successful import (, releases, release build): numbers names, taxa, synonyms, vernacular names, distributions, references , including -rank -gazetteer breakdowns.

title: Dataset metrics — clb_dataset_metrics
Dataset metrics — clb_dataset_metrics
text: dataset Dataset key alias. Defaults "3LXR". .raw Return raw parsed JSON instead tibble?

title: Dataset metrics — clb_dataset_metrics
text: one-row tibble metrics. Breakdown maps usagesByRankCount returned list-columns. NULL (message) dataset import yet.

title: Dataset metrics — clb_dataset_metrics
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ clb_dataset_metrics()         # latest extended COL release clb_dataset_metrics("COL25") } # }

title: Search datasets in ChecklistBank — clb_dataset_search
text: Full-text faceted search across public datasets ChecklistBank. Uses light dataset representation (full = FALSE) speed.

title: Search datasets in ChecklistBank — clb_dataset_search
code: clb_dataset_search(   q = NULL,   type = NULL,   origin = NULL,   ...,   limit = 50L,   max = limit )

title: Search datasets in ChecklistBank — clb_dataset_search
text: q Free-text query (matches title, alias, description, ...). Optional. type Filter dataset type (e.g. "taxonomic", "nomenclatural"). origin Filter origin (e.g. "external", "project", "release", "xrelease"). ... query parameters forwarded /dataset endpoint (e.g. contributesTo, code, gbifKey). limit Page size per request. max Maximum number datasets return. Use Inf fetch .

title: Search datasets in ChecklistBank — clb_dataset_search
text: clb object whose $data tibble datasets, one row , columns key, alias, title, type, origin, license issued.

title: Search datasets in ChecklistBank — clb_dataset_search

what: Match a scientific name against a ChecklistBank dataset — clb_match
title: Match a scientific name against a ChecklistBank dataset — clb_match
text: Looks single best-matching name usage scientific name dataset, defaulting latest monthly extended Catalogue Life release ("3LXR"). Catalogue Life analogue GBIF's name backbone matching.

title: Match a scientific name against a ChecklistBank dataset — clb_match
code: clb_match(   name,   authorship = NULL,   rank = NULL,   code = NULL,   dataset = "3LXR",   server = NULL,   .raw = FALSE )

title: Match a scientific name against a ChecklistBank dataset — clb_match
text: name Scientific name match. May include authorship, supply separately via authorship. authorship Optional authorship string. rank Optional rank disambiguate (e.g. "species", "genus"). code Optional nomenclatural code ("zoological", "botanical", "bacterial", "virus", ...). dataset Dataset key alias match . Defaults "3LXR". See clb_col_release() COL release aliases. server Optional base URL alternative matching service, e.g. locally running dockerized matching container ("http://localhost:8080"). Overrides CLB_BASE_URL call. .raw Return raw parsed JSON response instead tibble?

title: Match a scientific name against a ChecklistBank dataset — clb_match
text: one-row tibble match outcome (match, match_type), matched usage (usage_id, name, authorship, rank, status, ...) classification list-column. nothing matches, match FALSE usage columns NA.

title: Match a scientific name against a ChecklistBank dataset — clb_match
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ clb_match("Panthera leo") clb_match("Abies alba", rank = "species", code = "botanical") clb_match("Felis catus", dataset = "COL25") } # }

what: Match a checklist of names in bulk — clb_match_checklist
title: Match a checklist of names in bulk — clb_match_checklist
text: Matches many names , issuing requests parallel. Input columns echoed back row verbatim_ prefix results stay aligned source.

title: Match a checklist of names in bulk — clb_match_checklist
code: clb_match_checklist(   data,   name = "name",   authorship = "authorship",   rank = "rank",   code = "code",   dataset = "3LXR",   server = NULL,   max_active = 5L )

title: Match a checklist of names in bulk — clb_match_checklist
text: data character vector names, data frame. data frame, columns named name, authorship, rank code used (missing ones ignored). name, authorship, rank, code Column names data (data data frame) holding respective fields. dataset Dataset key alias match . Defaults "3LXR". server Optional alternative matching service base URL (see clb_match()). max_active Maximum number simultaneous HTTP requests.

title: Match a checklist of names in bulk — clb_match_checklist
text: tibble one row per input, verbatim_* input columns first followed match result columns clb_match().

title: Match a checklist of names in bulk — clb_match_checklist
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ clb_match_checklist(c("Panthera leo", "Bufo bufo", "Abies alba"))  df <- data.frame(   sciname = c("Puma concolor", "Vulpes vulpes"),   rank = c("species", "species") ) clb_match_checklist(df, name = "sciname", rank = "rank") } # }

what: Match a name and return all candidate usages — clb_match_verbose
title: Match a name and return all candidate usages — clb_match_verbose
text: Like clb_match() requests verbose output returns matched usage together alternative candidates, one per row.

title: Match a name and return all candidate usages — clb_match_verbose
code: clb_match_verbose(   name,   authorship = NULL,   rank = NULL,   code = NULL,   dataset = "3LXR",   server = NULL,   .raw = FALSE )

title: Match a name and return all candidate usages — clb_match_verbose
text: name Scientific name match. May include authorship, supply separately via authorship. authorship Optional authorship string. rank Optional rank disambiguate (e.g. "species", "genus"). code Optional nomenclatural code ("zoological", "botanical", "bacterial", "virus", ...). dataset Dataset key alias match . Defaults "3LXR". server Optional base URL alternative matching service. .raw Return raw parsed JSON response instead tibble?

title: Match a name and return all candidate usages — clb_match_verbose
text: tibble candidate usages logical primary column flagging chosen match. Zero rows nothing matched.

title: Match a name and return all candidate usages — clb_match_verbose

what: Parse values with a ChecklistBank value parser — clb_parse
title: Parse values with a ChecklistBank value parser — clb_parse
text: Runs one controlled-value parsers (see clb_parsers()) vector strings, e.g. normalise rank abbreviations, country names, dates license strings.

title: Parse values with a ChecklistBank value parser — clb_parse
Parse values with a ChecklistBank value parser — clb_parse
text: type Parser type, one clb_parsers() (e.g. "rank", "country", "date", "language", "license", "nomcode", "taxonomicstatus"). q character vector values parse. .raw Return raw parsed JSON instead tibble?

title: Parse values with a ChecklistBank value parser — clb_parse
text: tibble columns original, parsed parsable, one row per input value.

title: Parse values with a ChecklistBank value parser — clb_parse
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ clb_parse("rank", c("spec", "fam.", "ssp")) clb_parse("country", c("Deutschland", "The Netherlands")) } # }

title: Parse scientific names — clb_parse_name
text: Atomises one scientific names nomenclatural components (genus, epithets, rank, authorship, ...) using GBIF name parser exposed ChecklistBank.

title: Parse scientific names — clb_parse_name
code: clb_parse_name(name, authorship = NULL, rank = NULL, code = NULL, .raw = FALSE)

title: Parse scientific names — clb_parse_name
text: name character vector scientific names (may include authorship). authorship Optional authorship string (used single name supplied without author). rank Optional rank hint (e.g. "species"). code Optional nomenclatural code hint. .raw Return raw parsed JSON instead tibble?

title: Parse scientific names — clb_parse_name
text: tibble one row per input name columns scientificName, authorship, rank, genus, specificEpithet, type parsed. Nested fields (e.g. combinationAuthorship) returned list-columns.

title: Parse scientific names — clb_parse_name
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ clb_parse_name("Abies alba Mill.") clb_parse_name(c("Panthera leo (Linnaeus, 1758)", "Bellis perennis L.")) } # }

what: List the available ChecklistBank parsers — clb_parsers
title: List the available ChecklistBank parsers — clb_parsers
text: ChecklistBank exposes family parsers interpret free text controlled values (ranks, countries, dates, licenses, nomenclatural codes, taxonomic status, ). returns names value parsers usable clb_parse(). scientific-name parser wrapped separately clb_parse_name().

title: List the available ChecklistBank parsers — clb_parsers
List the available ChecklistBank parsers — clb_parsers
text: character vector parser type names.

title: List the available ChecklistBank parsers — clb_parsers

what: Autocomplete suggestions for name usages — clb_suggest
title: Autocomplete suggestions for name usages — clb_suggest
text: Fast prefix-based suggestions, suitable type-ahead lookups.

title: Autocomplete suggestions for name usages — clb_suggest
Autocomplete suggestions for name usages — clb_suggest
text: q Partial name complete. dataset Dataset key alias. Defaults "3LXR". ... query parameters (e.g. rank, status). .raw Return raw parsed JSON instead tibble?

title: Autocomplete suggestions for name usages — clb_suggest
text: tibble suggestions columns suggestion, usageId, rank, status group.

title: Autocomplete suggestions for name usages — clb_suggest
Synonyms of a taxon — clb_synonyms
text: Synonyms taxon.

title: Synonyms of a taxon — clb_synonyms
Synonyms of a taxon — clb_synonyms
text: id Taxon id within dataset. dataset Dataset key alias. Defaults "3LXR". .raw Return raw parsed JSON instead tibble?

title: Synonyms of a taxon — clb_synonyms
text: tibble synonym usages synonym_type column ("homotypic" "heterotypic") usual flattened usage columns. Zero rows taxon synonyms.

title: Synonyms of a taxon — clb_synonyms
Navigate the taxonomic tree — clb_tree
text: Without id, returns root taxa dataset's tree. id, returns path (lineage) root taxon. Use clb_children() descend one level.

title: Navigate the taxonomic tree — clb_tree
code: clb_tree(   dataset = "3LXR",   id = NULL,   extinct = TRUE,   ...,   limit = 100L,   max = limit,   .raw = FALSE )

title: Navigate the taxonomic tree — clb_tree
text: dataset Dataset key alias. Defaults "3LXR". id Optional taxon id. omitted tree roots returned. extinct Logical; include extinct taxa. Defaults TRUE. ... query parameters (e.g. insertPlaceholder). limit Page size listing roots. max Maximum number roots return. .raw Return raw parsed JSON instead tibble?

title: Navigate the taxonomic tree — clb_tree
text: tibble tree nodes columns id, name, authorship, rank, status, childCount count.

title: Navigate the taxonomic tree — clb_tree
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ clb_tree(dataset = "3LR")            # kingdoms / roots clb_tree(dataset = "3LR", id = "4CGXP")  # lineage of a taxon } # }

what: Get a name usage (taxon or synonym) by id — clb_usage
title: Get a name usage (taxon or synonym) by id — clb_usage
text: Get name usage (taxon synonym) id.

title: Get a name usage (taxon or synonym) by id — clb_usage
Get a name usage (taxon or synonym) by id — clb_usage
text: id Usage id within dataset. dataset Dataset key alias. Defaults "3LXR". .raw Return raw parsed JSON instead tibble?

title: Get a name usage (taxon or synonym) by id — clb_usage
text: one-row tibble usage's id, scientific_name, authorship, rank, status, label, parent_id full nested name list-column.

title: Get a name usage (taxon or synonym) by id — clb_usage
Metrics for a taxon — clb_usage_metrics
text: Returns subtree metrics taxon: tree depth, child species counts, -rank breakdown descendants.

title: Metrics for a taxon — clb_usage_metrics
Metrics for a taxon — clb_usage_metrics
text: id Taxon id within dataset. dataset Dataset key alias. Defaults "3LXR". .raw Return raw parsed JSON instead tibble?

title: Metrics for a taxon — clb_usage_metrics
text: one-row tibble metrics. Map fields taxaByRankCount returned list-columns.

title: Metrics for a taxon — clb_usage_metrics

what: Full-text search of name usages — clb_usage_search
title: Full-text search of name usages — clb_usage_search
text: Searches name usages within dataset (defaults latest extended COL release). accepted/synonym usage fields hoisted top-level columns; taxonomic classification full name object kept list-columns.

title: Full-text search of name usages — clb_usage_search
code: clb_usage_search(   q = NULL,   dataset = "3LXR",   rank = NULL,   status = NULL,   ...,   limit = 50L,   max = limit )  clb_search(   q = NULL,   dataset = "3LXR",   rank = NULL,   status = NULL,   ...,   limit = 50L,   max = limit )

title: Full-text search of name usages — clb_usage_search
text: q Free-text query. Optional (omit browse filters ). dataset Dataset key alias. Defaults "3LXR". rank Filter rank (e.g. "species"). status Filter taxonomic status (e.g. "accepted", "synonym"). ... query parameters forwarded search endpoint (e.g. extinct, nomCode, minRank, maxRank, type). limit Page size per request. max Maximum number usages return. Use Inf fetch .

title: Full-text search of name usages — clb_usage_search
text: clb object: list $data (tibble usages) $meta (total).

title: Full-text search of name usages — clb_usage_search
Vernacular (common) names — clb_vernacular
text: Looks vernacular names either single taxon (id given) across dataset free-text query.

title: Vernacular (common) names — clb_vernacular
code: clb_vernacular(   id = NULL,   dataset = "3LXR",   q = NULL,   lang = NULL,   ...,   limit = 50L,   max = limit,   .raw = FALSE )

title: Vernacular (common) names — clb_vernacular
text: id Optional taxon id. supplied, returns vernacular names taxon; otherwise performs dataset-wide vernacular search using q. dataset Dataset key alias. Defaults "3LXR". q Free-text query dataset-wide search (ignored id supplied). lang Optional ISO language filter (e.g. "eng", "deu"). ... query parameters. limit Page size dataset-wide search. max Maximum rows dataset-wide search. .raw Return raw parsed JSON instead tibble?

title: Vernacular (common) names — clb_vernacular
text: tibble vernacular names columns name, latin, language taxonID.

title: Vernacular (common) names — clb_vernacular

what: rcol: R Client for the Catalogue of Life / ChecklistBank API — rcol-package
title: rcol: R Client for the Catalogue of Life / ChecklistBank API — rcol-package
text: Access Catalogue Life (COL) ChecklistBank (CLB) web API. Match scientific names public dataset latest COL release, look datasets, taxa name usages, navigate taxonomic tree, run ChecklistBank name value parsers, discover latest annual monthly COL base extended releases.

title: rcol: R Client for the Catalogue of Life / ChecklistBank API — rcol-package
text: Maintainer: Markus Döring mdoering@gbif.org (ORCID) contributors: Catalogue Life [copyright holder, funder]

title: rcol 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2021-02-05 Initial release. Name matching dataset COL release: clb_match(), clb_match_verbose(), clb_match_checklist(). COL release discovery: clb_col_release(), clb_col_releases() covering monthly base (3LR), monthly extended (3LXR) annual (COL<YY>) releases. Parsers: clb_parsers(), clb_parse_name(), clb_parse(). Datasets: clb_dataset_search(), clb_dataset(), clb_dataset_metrics(). Name usages taxa: clb_usage(), clb_usage_search() / clb_search(), clb_suggest(), clb_classification(), clb_synonyms(), clb_vernacular(), clb_usage_metrics(). Tree navigation: clb_tree(), clb_children().
